scenario_name: Embedded AI feature inside a multi-tenant SaaS product
description: A multi-tenant SaaS vendor adds an AI feature to its product — a 'summarize the open record' or 'draft from these notes' affordance available to every customer. The feature is implemented once for all tenants and runs against per-tenant data. The dominant risk is cross-tenant context bleed: any flaw in tenant scoping (cache keys, RAG filters, memory store, embedding-collection scope) becomes a multi-customer data-disclosure incident.
assumptions: Hosted LLM under enterprise data-isolation contract.
Tenant identity is enforced at the API gateway and threaded through every retrieval and tool call.
Some legacy tenants exist on a per-tenant database; newer tenants share a multi-tenant DB with row-level security.

components.description: Per-tenant retrieval over the tenant's records.
Multi-tenant vector store with per-tenant collection scope.
Tenant-scoping validation classifier on every retrieval.
Vendor IDP issuing tokens carrying tenant attribute.
Audit logs include tenant ID on every record; SIEM-ingestion role-gated.

risks_identified.rationale: Cross-tenant context bleed is the dominant risk class for this archetype; any flaw in scoping affects all tenants.
risks_identified.residual_rationale: Per-tenant retrieval filter at every layer; tenant-scoping classifier validates filter presence.

risks_identified.rationale: Every retrieval and generation touches tenant-customer PII.
risks_identified.residual_rationale: Per-tenant scope; output PII redaction; processor terms with the LLM provider.

risks_identified.rationale: Tenant tokens compromise leads to read access across the tenant's records.
risks_identified.residual_rationale: Short-lived per-session tokens; tenant ID enforced server-side, not client-trusted.

risks_identified.rationale: Every tenant brings its own data-protection regime; vendor must support GDPR, CCPA, HIPAA (some tenants), and sub-processor disclosure.
risks_identified.residual_rationale: DPA terms, sub-processor list, BAA available for HIPAA tenants.

risks_identified.rationale: Shared vector store across many tenants; isolation failures are multi-customer events.
risks_identified.residual_rationale: Per-tenant collections; vector-store admin access tightly audited.

risks_identified.rationale: Centralized logging across all tenants risks broad disclosure if access controls fail.
risks_identified.residual_rationale: Logs partitioned by tenant; SIEM access role-gated.

risks_identified.rationale: All tenants degrade simultaneously when the AI feature is degraded.
risks_identified.residual_rationale: Feature-flag rollback per tenant; non-AI fallback paths.

existing_controls.description: Tenant-scoping validation classifier asserts a per-tenant filter on every retrieval.
Per-tenant vector store collections; admin access audited.
Tenant ID enforced server-side at the API gateway, not trusted from client.
DPA + sub-processor list + BAA available; per-tenant data-residency where contracted.
Logs partitioned by tenant; SIEM access role-gated.
Feature-flag rollback per tenant on AI-feature degradation.

control_gaps.description: No automated cross-tenant retrieval chaos test in CI.
control_gaps.suggested_remediation: Add a CI test that asserts a tenant-A user cannot retrieve tenant-B content even when forced; fail the build on any regression.

control_gaps.description: Embedding-store admin-access trails are audited but not anomaly-detected.
control_gaps.suggested_remediation: Add anomaly detection on vector-store admin operations; alert on cross-tenant access patterns.

control_gaps.description: Legacy tenants on per-tenant DB and newer tenants on shared DB have inconsistent isolation guarantees.
control_gaps.suggested_remediation: Document the isolation model per tenant; communicate the gap to enterprise customers.
